data_preparation.modules¶

data_preparation.modules.constants¶

Constants for electric truck data preparation.

This module defines column names, label encoders, signal mappings, weather labels, highway labels, highway speed ranges, CRS codes, and UEA dataset settings used throughout the data preparation pipeline.

Example

>>> from constants import AIR_TEMPERATURE_LABEL_ENCODER
>>> AIR_TEMPERATURE_LABEL_ENCODER["cold"]
2
data_preparation.modules.constants.WEATHER_COLUMNS¶

Columns related to weather labeling.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_COLUMNS¶

Columns related to highway labeling.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.AIR_TEMPERATURE_LABEL_ENCODER¶

Mapping of air temperature labels to integers.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.WEATHER_LABEL_ENCODER¶

Mapping of weather condition labels to integers.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_LABEL_ENCODER¶

Mapping of highway labels to integers.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_OLD_TO_NEW¶

Old-to-new highway label integer mapping.

Type:

dict[int, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_NEW_TO_OLD¶

New-to-old highway label integer mapping.

Type:

dict[int, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.WEATHER_OLD_TO_NEW¶

Old-to-new weather label integer mapping.

Type:

dict[int, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.WEATHER_NEW_TO_OLD¶

New-to-old weather label integer mapping.

Type:

dict[int, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.LABEL_ENCODERS¶

Label encoders for all relevant columns.

Type:

dict[str, dict[str, int]]

data_preparation.modules.constants.LABEL_DECODERS¶

Label decoders for all relevant columns.

Type:

dict[str, dict[int, str]]

data_preparation.modules.constants.MERGE_SIGNALS¶

Signal column names used for merging datasets.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.SIGNAL_MAPPING¶

Mapping of all signal names to column indices.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.TRAIN_SIGNAL_MAPPING¶

Subset of signals used for model training.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.WEATHER_LABELING_SIGNALS¶

Signal columns used for weather labeling.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.AIR_TEMPERATURE_LABELS¶

Ordered list of air temperature label strings.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.WEATHER_LABELS¶

Ordered list of weather condition label strings.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.METEOSTAT_CODES¶

Mapping of Meteostat integer codes to descriptions. Reference: https://dev.meteostat.net/formats.

Type:

dict[int, str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.METEOSTAT_TO_WEATHER¶

Mapping of Meteostat codes to weather labels.

Type:

dict[int, str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_LABELING_SIGNALS¶

Signal columns used for highway labeling.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_LABELS¶

Ordered list of OSM highway label strings. Reference: https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:highway.

Type:

list[str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.TRUCK_SPEED_RANGES¶

Speed ranges (min, max) per highway type. Reference: https://dhl-freight-connections.com/en/business/truck-speed-limits-europe/.

Type:

dict[str, tuple[int, int]]

data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_PRIORITY¶

Priority ranking of OSM highway types.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_preparation.modules.constants.COUNTRY_CODE_TO_OSM_MAP¶

Mapping of ISO country codes to OSM PBF filenames.

Type:

dict[str, str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.CRS_CODES¶

EPSG CRS codes per country and multi-country key. Reference: https://epsg.io/.

Type:

dict[str, str]

data_preparation.modules.constants.UEA¶

Per-dataset configuration for UEA archive datasets. Reference: https://arxiv.org/abs/1811.00075.

Type:

dict[str, dict[str, int]]

data_preparation.modules.highway_labeling¶

Labeling GPS trajectory points with highway types using OpenStreetMap (OSM).

This module loads verified trip datasets, assigns highway labels to each GPS point using spatial matching against offline or online OSM sources, and writes the labeled outputs and verification plots to disk.

Example

>>> labeler = TruckDataHighwayLabeler(cfg)
>>> labeler.run()
class data_preparation.modules.highway_labeling.TruckDataHighwayLabeler(cfg: DictConfig)[source]¶

Bases: object

Labels GPS trajectory points of electric trucks with OSM highway types.

Loads verified electric truck trip datasets, spatially matches each GPS point against offline or online OpenStreetMap road networks, and assigns the appropriate highway type label (e.g. motorway, trunk, residential). Labeled outputs are saved to disk together with interactive Folium verification maps.

cfg¶

Hydra configuration object. Must contain a data_preparation sub-config with the fields file_extension, verified_data_dir, labeled_data_dir, plots_dir, osm_maps_dir, trip_locations_file and processed_osm_maps_dir.

Type:

DictConfig

verified_data_dir¶

Directory containing verified parquet trip files.

Type:

Path

file_extension¶

File suffix used when globbing trip files (e.g. '.parquet').

Type:

str

labeled_data_dir¶

Directory where labeled parquet files are written.

Type:

Path

plots_dir¶

Directory where Folium HTML verification maps are written.

Type:

Path

trip_locations_file¶

Path to the JSON file mapping country keys to trip filenames created by data_understanding.modules.analysis.TruckDataAnalyzer.get_trip_locations().

Type:

Path

osm_maps_dir¶

Root directory containing the offline OSM PBF maps. Reference: https://download.geofabrik.de.

Type:

Path

processed_osm_maps_dir¶

Cache directory for processed highway GeoDataFrames.

Type:

Path

highway_labeling_signals¶

Signals used for highway labeling.

Type:

list[str]

highway_labels¶

List of OSM highway labels.

Type:

list[str]

truck_speed_ranges¶

Recommended speed ranges per highway type.

Type:

dict[str, tuple[int, int]]

highway_priority¶

Priority ranking for conflicting highway assignments.

Type:

dict[str, int]

country_code_to_osm_map¶

Mapping from country codes to OSM PBF filenames.

Type:

dict[str, str]

crs_codes¶

CRS projection codes per country.

Type:

dict[str, str]

static add_empty_highway_column(df: DataFrame | GeoDataFrame) → DataFrame | GeoDataFrame[source]¶

Returns a copy of df with a highway_label column initialized to None.

Used as a safe fallback whenever a labeling step fails or produces no results.

Parameters:

df (pd.Dataframe | gpd.GeoDataFrame) – Input trip DataFrame.

Returns:

A copy of df with an additional highway_label column set to None.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame | gpd.GeoDataFrame

calculate_weighted_score(gdf_labeled: GeoDataFrame, max_distance: int) → Series[source]¶

Computes a weighted score for each candidate highway match.

The score combines three normalized sub-scores:
  • Speed score (weight 0.3): 1.0 if the point’s vehicle speed falls within the

    expected range for the candidate highway type, else 0.0.

  • Priority score (weight 0.3): highway priority value normalized to

    [0, 1] using data_preparation.modules.constants.HIGHWAY_PRIORITY.

  • Distance score (weight 0.4): linear decay from 1.0 (distance = 0)

    to 0.0 (distance = max_distance).

Parameters:

  • gdf_labeled (gpd.GeoDataFrame) – Candidate matches from a spatial join. Must contain vehspd_cval_cpc, highway, and dist_to_highway_m columns.

  • max_distance (int) – Maximum search radius in metres used during the spatial join; serves as the normalisation denominator for the distance score.

Returns:

Float scores in [0, 1] aligned to gdf_labeled’s index.

Return type:

pd.Series

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

get_datasets() → tuple[list[Path], list[str]][source]¶

Gets all trip files in the verified data directory.

Returns:

A tuple of (paths, filenames) where

paths is a sorted list of pathlib.Path objects matching file_extension, and filenames contains the corresponding stems (no extension).

Return type:

tuple[list[Path], list[str]]

get_datasets_by_country() → dict[str, tuple[list[Path], list[str]]][source]¶

Groups trip file paths by country (or country combination) key.

Reads the JSON file at trip_locations_path which maps each country key to a list of trip filenames, then resolves absolute paths for every filename.

Returns:

A dict mapping each country key (e.g. "DE" or "DK,SE") to a tuple (paths, filenames) of resolved Path objects and their stems.

Return type:

dict[str, tuple[list[Path], list[str]]]

load_multiple_offline_maps_with_pyogrio(country: str) → GeoDataFrame[source]¶

Loads and concatenates highway layers for a comma-separated list of countries.

Delegates to load_offline_map_with_pyogrio() for each individual country, then concatenates the results into a single GeoDataFrame.

Parameters:

country (list[str]) – Comma-separated country codes (e.g. "DK,SE").

Returns:

A geopandas GeoDataFrame covering all specified countries, with a reset integer index.

Return type:

gpd.GeoDataFrame

load_offline_map_with_pyogrio(country: str) → GeoDataFrame[source]¶

Loads the highway layer for a single country from its offline OSM file.

On the first call the layer is read from the GeoPackage / PBF via pyogrio and cached as a Parquet file. Subsequent calls return the cached Parquet directly, skipping the expensive file read.

Parameters:

country (str) – Country code (e.g. "DE"). Used to look up the OSM filename in country_osm_map and to name the cache file.

Returns:

A geopandas GeoDataFrame containing highway geometries and a highway attribute column, filtered to the types listed in highway_labels.

Return type:

gpd.GeoDataFrame

Raises:

  • Exception – Re-raises any exception thrown by pyogrio after logging

  • the error. –

offline_labeling_pipeline(bbox_buffer: float, max_distance: float, function: callable) → None[source]¶

Runs an offline labeling function over all countries and their trip files.

Groups trips by country using get_datasets_by_country(), loads the corresponding offline highway map once per country (or country combination), then applies function to each individual trip. Results are written to labeled_data_dir and a verification map is generated. The highway GeoDataFrame is released from memory after each country to limit peak RAM usage.

Parameters:

  • bbox_buffer (float) – Padding in decimal degrees added to the trip’s bounding box when clipping the highway GeoDataFrame.

  • max_distance (float) – Maximum snap distance in metres passed to function.

  • function – A callable with signature (df, bbox_buffer, max_distance, crs, gdf_highways) -> pd.DataFrame that performs the actual labeling (e.g. offline_labeling_with_pyogrio()).

offline_labeling_with_pyogrio(df: DataFrame, bbox_buffer: float, max_distance: float, crs: str, gdf_highways: GeoDataFrame) → DataFrame[source]¶

Labels GPS points against a pre-loaded offline highway GeoDataFrame.

Clips the highway GeoDataFrame to the trip’s bounding box, projects both points and highways to the specified CRS, then runs a nearest- neighbour spatial join. Duplicate matches are resolved by the composite weighted score from calculate_weighted_score().

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame with latitude_cval_ippc and longitude_cval_ippc columns.

  • bbox_buffer (float) – Padding in decimal degrees applied to the trip extent when clipping the highway GeoDataFrame.

  • max_distance (float) – Maximum snap distance in metres passed to gpd.sjoin_nearest().

  • crs (str) – EPSG string for the projected CRS used during distance calculations (e.g. "EPSG:3035").

  • gdf_highways (gpd.GeoDataFrame) – Pre-loaded highway GeoDataFrame in EPSG:4326.

Returns:

A copy of df with highway_label and dist_to_highway_m columns. Falls back to highway_label = None on any error.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

offline_labeling_with_pyrosm(df: DataFrame, bbox_buffer: float, max_distance: float, country: str) → DataFrame[source]¶

Labels GPS points using pyrosm to parse an offline OSM PBF file.

Reads the bounding-box-clipped highway network from the OSM PBF for the given country, then performs a priority-weighted nearest-neighbour join to assign highway labels.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame with latitude_cval_ippc and longitude_cval_ippc columns.

  • bbox_buffer (float) – Padding in decimal degrees added to the trip’s bounding box before passing it to pyrosm.

  • max_distance (float) – Maximum snap distance in metres passed to geopandas.sjoin_nearest().

  • country (str) – Country code used to look up the OSM PBF filename and the projected CRS.

Returns:

A copy of df with a highway_label column and the temporary dist_to_highway_m column dropped. Falls back to highway_label = None on any error.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

online_labeling_pipeline(bbox_buffer: float, max_distance: float, country: str, chunk_size: int, function: callable, datasets: tuple[list[Path], list[str]]) → None[source]¶

Runs an online labeling function over a list of trip files.

Iterates over the provided file paths, skips already-processed outputs, and calls function to label each trip. Trips larger than chunk_size rows are split into chunks that are labeled individually and then concatenated. A verification map is generated for every trip.

Parameters:

  • bbox_buffer (float) – Padding in decimal degrees added to each trip’s bounding box before the online query.

  • max_distance (float) – Maximum snap distance in metres.

  • country (str) – Country code forwarded to function and to the plot directory hierarchy.

  • chunk_size (int) – Maximum number of rows to process in a single call to function. Larger files are split into chunks of this size.

  • function – A callable with signature (df, bbox_buffer, max_distance, country) -> pd.DataFrame that performs the actual labeling (e.g. online_labeling_with_overpass()).

  • datasets (tuple[list[Path], list[str]]) – Tuple (paths, filenames) as returned by get_datasets() or get_datasets_by_country().

online_labeling_with_osmnx_features(df: DataFrame, bbox_buffer: float, max_distance: float, country: str) → DataFrame[source]¶

Labels GPS points using highway features fetched live from OSM via osmnx.

Constructs a bounding box from the trip’s coordinate extent (plus bbox_buffer), downloads matching highway features with osmnx.features.features_from_bbox(), then performs a nearest- neighbour spatial join within max_distance to assign a label to each point.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame with latitude_cval_ippc and longitude_cval_ippc columns.

  • bbox_buffer (float) – Padding in decimal degrees added to each side of the bounding box before querying OSM.

  • max_distance (float) – Maximum snap distance in metres. Points further from any highway remain unlabeled (NaN).

  • country (str) – Country code used for CRS lookup.

Returns:

A copy of df with a highway_label column. If the OSM request or spatial join fails, all labels are None.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

online_labeling_with_osmnx_graph(df: DataFrame, bbox_buffer: float = 0.01) → DataFrame[source]¶

Labels GPS points by snapping them to the nearest edge of an OSM street graph.

Downloads a routable graph for the trip’s bounding box using osmnx.graph.graph_from_bbox(), then snaps every GPS point to its nearest graph edge with osmnx.distance.nearest_edges() to extract the highway type.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame with latitude_cval_ippc and longitude_cval_ippc columns.

  • bbox_buffer (float) – Padding in decimal degrees added to each side of the bounding box before downloading the graph. Defaults to 0.01.

Returns:

A copy of df with a highway_label column. If graph download fails or no edges are found, all labels are None.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of ChatGPT (OpenAI).

online_labeling_with_overpass(df: DataFrame, bbox_buffer: float, max_distance: float, country: str) → DataFrame[source]¶

Labels GPS points using highway geometries fetched from the Overpass API.

Queries the Overpass API for all highway ways within the trip’s bounding box, builds a GeoDataFrame of road geometries, and then scores every (point, candidate highway) pair using a combination of priority and distance. Also detects roundabouts and intersections.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame with latitude_cval_ippc, longitude_cval_ippc, and vehspd_cval_cpc columns.

  • bbox_buffer (float) – Padding in decimal degrees added to each side of the bounding box before querying Overpass.

  • max_distance (float) – Maximum snap distance in metres. Matches beyond this threshold are discarded.

  • country (str) – Country code used for CRS projection lookup.

Returns:

A copy of df with four additional columns:

  • highway_label – OSM highway type string or None.

  • is_roundabout – True if the matched way is a roundabout.

  • is_intersection – True if ≥ 3 distinct OSM ways are within 20 m.

  • dist_to_highway_m – Distance in metres to the matched highway.

Falls back to a single highway_label = None column on any error.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

plot_and_verify_labels(df: DataFrame, filename: str, country: str) → None[source]¶

Renders an interactive Folium map visualising the highway labels for one trip.

Each trajectory segment is coloured by its assigned highway type. A legend shows the distribution of labels as percentages. The map is written to disk as an HTML file.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Labeled trip DataFrame. Must contain latitude_cval_ippc, longitude_cval_ippc, and highway_label columns.

  • filename (str) – Stem of the source file, which is used as the map title and output filename.

  • country (str) – Country key used to create a country-level sub-directory under plots_dir.

Raises:

ValueError – If df is empty.

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

run() → None[source]¶

Entry point for the highway labeling pipeline.

Executes the labeling pipeline and logs the total elapsed time.

Note

offline_labeling_pipeline() can be replaced by online_labeling_pipeline()

setup_dirs() → None[source]¶

Creates required output directories if they do not already exist.

data_preparation.modules.preparation¶

Data preparation pipeline for truck sensor time-series classification.

This module provides TruckDataPreparator, which orchestrates the full preprocessing pipeline from raw verified trip files to normalised, split, and windowed NumPy arrays ready for model training.

The pipeline consists of four sequential stages:

  1. Label merging – joins highway and weather label files onto the verified trip data and validates the resulting feature set.

  2. Window extraction – applies a sliding window over each labeled trip and retains only windows where every time step shares the same label.

  3. Filtering, splitting, and normalisation – drops configured signals and label classes, performs stratified train / val / test splitting, fits a StandardScaler on training features, and creates a class-balanced few-shot training subset.

  4. Reporting – saves per-split label distribution plots (PNG + PDF) and JSON summaries to the results directory.

Expected on-disk layout after stage 3:

<data_split_dir>/
└── <window_size>/
    └── <label_col>/
        ├── train.npy
        ├── fewshot_train.npy
        ├── val.npy
        └── test.npy

Each .npy file has shape (B, T, C+1) where B is the number of windows, T is the window size, C is the number of feature channels, and the last channel holds the integer-encoded class label.

Example

>>> preparator = TruckDataPreparator(cfg)
>>> preparator.run()
class data_preparation.modules.preparation.TruckDataPreparator(cfg: DictConfig)[source]¶

Bases: object

Orchestrates the full truck sensor data preparation pipeline.

Reads configuration from a Hydra/OmegaConf DictConfig, resolves all input/output paths, and exposes the four pipeline stages as public methods. Call run() to execute the complete pipeline.

cfg¶

Hydra configuration object. All required keys are read from cfg.data_preparation.

Type:

DictConfig

Example

>>> from omegaconf import OmegaConf
>>> cfg = OmegaConf.load("configs/data_preparation.yaml")
>>> preparator = TruckDataPreparator(cfg)
>>> preparator.run()
compute_label_distribution(data: ndarray, label_col: str, filename: str, save_path: Path) → None[source]¶

Plots and saves the label distribution for a window array.

Decodes integer labels using self.label_decoders, computes per-class counts and percentage proportions, renders a horizontal bar chart, and writes both a PNG (150 dpi) and a JSON summary to save_path.

Parameters:

  • data (np.ndarray) – Window array of shape (B, T, C+1); the label is read from data[:, 0, -1].

  • label_col (str) – Label column name used to look up the decoder in self.label_decoders.

  • filename (str) – Base name (without extension) for the output files.

  • save_path (Path) – Directory where the PNG and JSON are written. Must already exist.

extract_and_save_windows() → None[source]¶

Extracts fixed-length windows from all labeled datasets.

Iterates over every combination of label column and window size defined in the configuration. For each combination, extract_windows_from_file() is called on every labeled file; valid window arrays are vertically stacked and saved as a single .npy file. A label distribution plot and JSON summary are also written via compute_label_distribution().

Files that produce no valid windows are silently skipped. Memory is explicitly freed between label/window-size iterations using gc.collect().

extract_windows_from_file(path: Path, columns: list[str], window_size: int) → ndarray | None[source]¶

Extracts valid non-overlapping windows from a single labeled file.

Loads the requested columns from a parquet file, verifies the feature ordering, integer-encodes the label column, and uses a sliding window view to identify positions where all window_size consecutive rows share the same label. Only every window_size-th valid start index is retained to avoid overlap. Windows containing NaN label values are subsequently removed.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Path to the labeled parquet file.

  • columns (list[str]) – Columns to load; the last element is treated as the label column.

  • window_size (int) – Number of consecutive rows per window.

Returns:

Float array of shape (num_valid_windows, window_size, num_features) containing valid windows, or None if the file has fewer rows than window_size or no valid windows remain after filtering.

Return type:

np.ndarray | None

Raises:

  • ValueError – If required signals are missing or unexpected columns are present during feature verification.

  • KeyError – If no label encoder is registered for the label column.

filter_split_and_normalize_windowed_data(random_seed: int = 42) → None[source]¶

Filters, stratifies, normalises, and persists windowed data splits.

For every window file in data_windows_dir:

  1. Drops configured feature signals (signals_to_drop).

  2. Filters out windows whose label belongs to labels_to_drop.

  3. Performs stratified per-class splitting into train / val / test according to train_pct, val_pct, and test_pct.

  4. Fits a StandardScaler on training features and applies it to all three splits (label column excluded).

  5. Creates a class-balanced few-shot subset from the training split using fewshot_pct.

  6. Saves all four arrays (train, fewshot_train, val, test) as .npy files under data_split_dir.

  7. Writes label distribution reports for every split via compute_label_distribution().

Parameters:

random_seed (int) – Seed passed to numpy.random.seed() for reproducible shuffling and few-shot sampling. Defaults to 42.

Raises:

AssertionError – If train_pct + val_pct + test_pct does not equal 1.0 (within 1e-6) or if fewshot_pct is outside (0, 1].

get_datasets(data_dir: Path, ext: str) → tuple[list[Path], list[str]][source]¶

Returns all matching files in a directory, sorted alphabetically.

Parameters:

  • data_dir (Path) – Directory to search for files.

  • ext (str) – File extension used to filter results (e.g. ".parquet").

Returns:

A 2-tuple (paths, filenames) where

  • paths – sorted list of Path objects whose names end with ext.

  • filenames – corresponding list of file stems (filename without extension).

Return type:

tuple[list[Path], list[str]]

merge_and_verify_label_datasets() → None[source]¶

Merges highway and weather label files with verified trip data.

For each trip file in verified_data_dir, loads the corresponding highway and weather label files, asserts equal row counts, merges on merge_signals, validates the resulting feature set via verify_expected_features(), and writes the merged file to labeled_data_dir.

Raises:

  • AssertionError – If row counts differ between the verified, highway, or weather DataFrames for any trip.

  • KeyError – If a trip filename present in verified_data_dir is not found in highway_labels_dir or weather_labels_dir.

plot_label_distributions(data: ndarray, label_col: str, save_path: Path, filename: str) → None[source]¶

Renders a publication-quality label distribution bar chart.

Decodes integer labels, sorts classes by descending count, renders a vertical bar chart with a log-scaled y-axis and an average-count reference line, and writes PNG (300 dpi), PDF, and a JSON summary to save_path. Uses Times New Roman font if the font files are present at the configured paths.

The figure width scales dynamically: num_classes * 1.2 inches for more than five classes, 7 inches otherwise.

Parameters:

  • data (np.ndarray) – Window array of shape (B, T, C+1); the label is read from data[:, 0, -1].

  • label_col (str) – Label column name used to look up the decoder in self.label_decoders.

  • save_path (Path) – Directory where PNG, PDF, and JSON files are written. Must already exist.

  • filename (str) – Base name (without extension) for all output files.

preparation_pipeline() → None[source]¶

Defines and executes the ordered data preparation stages.

Calls the four pipeline stages in sequence. Individual stages can be commented out to resume the pipeline from an intermediate checkpoint.

Stages (in order):

  1. merge_and_verify_label_datasets()

  2. extract_and_save_windows()

  3. _filter_split_and_normalize_windowed_data()

  4. _plot_all()

run() → None[source]¶

Executes the full data preparation pipeline and logs elapsed time.

Calls _preparation_pipeline(), wraps it with gc.collect() before and after, and logs the total wall-clock duration at INFO level.

setup_dirs() → None[source]¶

Creates all required output directories if they do not already exist.

Applies mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) to labeled_data_dir, data_windows_dir, results_dir, and data_split_dir.

verify_expected_features(df: DataFrame, verification_mapping: dict) → DataFrame[source]¶

Validates and reorders DataFrame columns against an expected mapping.

Checks that the DataFrame contains exactly the signals in verification_mapping — no more and no fewer — then reorders the columns to match the index order defined by the mapping values.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame whose columns are validated.

  • verification_mapping (dict[str, int]) – Mapping of expected signal names to their target column indices.

Returns:

The input DataFrame with columns reordered to match verification_mapping.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Raises:

  • ValueError – If any expected signals are absent from df.

  • ValueError – If any unexpected signals are present in df.

data_preparation.modules.truck_data_loaders¶

PyTorch Dataset and DataLoader factories for truck sensor classification data.

This module provides three classes for loading windowed truck sensor data stored as .npy files on disk:

  • TruckDataset – a map-style Dataset for deep-learning models.

  • TruckDataFactory – builds DataLoader instances from TruckDataset for training, few-shot training, validation, and testing.

  • TruckDataFactoryBenchmark – loads raw NumPy arrays for scikit-learn-compatible classifiers (ROCKET, InceptionTime, etc.).

The expected on-disk layout is:

<data_path>/
└── <window_size>/
    └── <label_col>/
        ├── train.npy
        ├── fewshot_train.npy
        ├── val.npy
        └── test.npy

Each .npy file has shape (B, T, C+1) where B is the number of windows, T is the sequence length (window size), C is the number of feature channels, and the last channel index holds the integer class label (constant across the time axis for each window).

Example

>>> from src.data_preparation.modules.truck_data_loaders import (
...     TruckDataset, TruckDataFactory, TruckDataFactoryBenchmark
... )
>>>
>>> # --- Deep-learning pipeline ---
>>> factory = TruckDataFactory(
...     batch_size=64,
...     num_workers=4,
...     data_path="data/truck",
...     window_size=128,
...     label_col="highway_label",
... )
>>> train_loader, fewshot_loader, val_loader, test_loader = (
...     factory.build_train_val_test_loaders()
... )
>>> x_batch, y_batch = next(iter(train_loader))
>>> x_batch.shape   # (64, 128, C)
>>> y_batch.shape   # (64,)
>>>
>>> # --- Benchmark / scikit-learn pipeline ---
>>> bench = TruckDataFactoryBenchmark(
...     data_path="data/truck",
...     window_size=128,
...     label_col="highway_label",
... )
>>> X_train, y_train, X_fs, y_fs, X_test, y_test = bench.get_splits()
>>> X_train.shape   # (B, C, T)  — channels-first for sktime/aeon
class data_preparation.modules.truck_data_loaders.TruckDataFactory(batch_size: int, num_workers: int, data_path: Path, window_size: int, label_col: str)[source]¶

Bases: object

Factory that creates DataLoader objects for truck data.

Wraps TruckDataset and exposes convenience methods for building individual loaders or the full set of train / few-shot-train / val / test loaders in a single call.

batch_size¶

Number of windows per mini-batch.

Type:

int

num_workers¶

Subprocesses spawned by each DataLoader for prefetching.

Type:

int

data_path¶

Root directory that contains the <window_size>/<label_col>/ sub-tree.

Type:

Path

window_size¶

Sequence length; selects the correct sub-directory under data_path.

Type:

int

label_col¶

Label column name forwarded to TruckDataset.

Type:

str

Example

>>> factory = TruckDataFactory(
...     batch_size=64,
...     num_workers=4,
...     data_path="data/truck",
...     window_size=128,
...     label_col="highway_label",
... )
>>> train_loader, fewshot_loader, val_loader, test_loader = (
...     factory.build_train_val_test_loaders()
... )
>>> x, y = next(iter(train_loader))
>>> x.shape  # torch.Size([64, 128, C])
build_loader(mode: str) → DataLoader[source]¶

Builds a single shuffled DataLoader.

Creates a TruckDataset for the requested split and wraps it in a DataLoader with pinned memory and persistent workers enabled for GPU training throughput.

Parameters:

mode (str) – Split identifier matching a .npy filename under the configured data path (e.g. "train", "val", "test", "fewshot_train").

Returns:

Configured loader that yields (x, y) batches with x of shape (batch_size, T, C) and y of shape (batch_size,).

Return type:

DataLoader

build_train_val_test_loaders() → tuple[DataLoader, DataLoader, DataLoader, DataLoader][source]¶

Builds all four standard data loaders in a single call.

Returns:

A 4-tuple (train_loader, fewshot_train_loader, val_loader, test_loader) where each element is a shuffled DataLoader for the corresponding split.

Return type:

tuple[DataLoader, DataLoader, DataLoader, DataLoader]

class data_preparation.modules.truck_data_loaders.TruckDataFactoryBenchmark(data_path: str | Path, window_size: int, label_col: str)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset

Loads truck sensor data as raw NumPy arrays for scikit-learn classifiers.

Unlike TruckDataFactory, this class does not produce PyTorch DataLoader objects. Instead it returns channels-first NumPy arrays compatible with sktime / aeon estimators such as RocketClassifier and InceptionTimeClassifier.

data_path¶

Root directory that contains the <window_size>/<label_col>/ sub-tree.

Type:

str | Path

window_size (int): Sequence length; selects the correct

sub-directory under data_path.

label_col (str): Label column name; must be one of

"highway_label" or "weather_label" for re-encoding to apply, otherwise raw integer labels are returned unchanged.

Example

>>> bench = TruckDataFactoryBenchmark(
...     data_path="data/truck",
...     window_size=128,
...     label_col="weather_label",
... )
>>> X_train, y_train, X_fs, y_fs, X_test, y_test = bench.get_splits()
>>> X_train.shape  # (B, C, T) — channels-first
>>> y_train.dtype  # dtype('int64')
get_splits() → tuple[ndarray, ndarray, ndarray, ndarray, ndarray, ndarray][source]¶

Loads the train, few-shot-train, and test splits in one call.

Returns:

A 6-tuple (X_train, y_train, X_fewshot_train, y_fewshot_train, X_test, y_test) where every X array has shape (B, C, T) and every y array has shape (B,).

Return type:

tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

load_data(mode: str) → tuple[ndarray, ndarray][source]¶

Loads a single split from disk and returns channels-first arrays.

Reads the .npy file for the requested split, transposes the feature array to channels-first order (B, C, T) using einops.rearrange(), extracts the scalar label for each window from the first time step, and applies label re-encoding if required.

Parameters:

mode (str) – Split identifier matching a .npy filename (e.g. "train", "fewshot_train", "test").

Returns:

A 2-tuple (X, y) where

  • X – float array of shape (B, C, T) containing the feature channels in channels-first order.

  • y – int array of shape (B,) holding the re-encoded class label for each window.

Return type:

tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]

class data_preparation.modules.truck_data_loaders.TruckDataset(data_path: str | Path, window_size: int, label_col: str, mode: str)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset

Map-style PyTorch Dataset for windowed truck sensor data.

Loads a memory-mapped .npy file on construction and returns (features, label) pairs indexed by window position. Integer labels are optionally re-encoded to consecutive integers via lookup tables defined in src.data_preparation.modules.constants.

data_path¶

Root directory that contains the <window_size>/<label_col>/ sub-tree.

Type:

str | Path

window_size¶

Sequence length used to locate the correct sub-directory and stored as an instance attribute.

Type:

int

label_col¶

Label column name; must be one of "highway_label" or "weather_label" for re-encoding to apply, otherwise raw integer labels are returned unchanged.

Type:

str

mode¶

Split name corresponding to the .npy filename (e.g. "train", "fewshot_train", "val", "test").

Type:

str

Example

>>> dataset = TruckDataset(
...     data_path="data/truck",
...     window_size=128,
...     label_col="highway_label",
...     mode="train",
... )
>>> x, y = dataset[0]
>>> x.shape   # torch.Size([128, C])
>>> y.item()  # integer class index

data_preparation.modules.uea_data_loaders¶

PyTorch Dataset and DataLoader factory for UEA/UCR multivariate time-series.

This module provides two classes for loading and normalising datasets from the UEA & UCR Time Series Classification repository via sktime.datasets.load_UCR_UEA_dataset():

  • UEADataset – a map-style Dataset that downloads, normalises, and label-encodes a single split ("train" or "test").

  • UEADataFactory – coordinates scaler sharing between the train and test splits and exposes convenience methods for building individual or paired DataLoader objects.

The expected normalisation contract is:

  1. A StandardScaler is fitted on the flattened training windows (B*T, C).

  2. The same fitted scaler is passed in when constructing the test UEADataset to prevent data leakage.

Example

>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> from src.data_preparation.modules.uea_data_loaders import UEADataFactory
>>>
>>> factory = UEADataFactory(
...     name="ArticularyWordRecognition",
...     data_path=Path("data/uea"),
...     batch_size=32,
...     num_workers=2,
... )
>>> train_loader, test_loader = factory.build_train_test_loaders()
>>> x_batch, y_batch = next(iter(train_loader))
>>> x_batch.shape   # (32, T, C)
>>> y_batch.shape   # (32,)
class data_preparation.modules.uea_data_loaders.UEADataFactory(name: str, data_path: Path, batch_size: int, num_workers: int)[source]¶

Bases: object

Factory that builds normalised DataLoaders for a single UEA/UCR dataset.

Manages scaler sharing between the training and test splits so that the caller never has to handle the fitted StandardScaler manually. The train loader must be built before the test loader; this is enforced automatically by build_train_test_loaders().

name¶

UEA/UCR dataset identifier passed to UEADataset.

Type:

str

data_path¶

Directory where sktime caches downloaded datasets.

Type:

Path

batch_size¶

Number of windows per mini-batch.

Type:

int

num_workers¶

Subprocesses spawned by each DataLoader for prefetching.

Type:

int

build_loader(split: str, shuffle: bool = True) → DataLoader | None[source]¶

Builds a DataLoader for one split.

Constructs a UEADataset, passing the stored train_scaler automatically for the "test" split. After a successful "train" build the fitted scaler is cached in self.train_scaler for subsequent test builds.

Any exception raised during dataset or loader construction is caught, logged at ERROR level, and None is returned so that the caller can skip the dataset gracefully.

Parameters:

  • split (str) – One of "train" or "test".

  • shuffle (bool) – Whether to shuffle the dataset each epoch. Defaults to True; set to False for evaluation splits.

Returns:

A configured DataLoader yielding (x, y) batches with x of shape (batch_size, T, C) and y of shape (batch_size,), or None if construction failed.

Return type:

DataLoader | None

build_train_test_loaders() → tuple[DataLoader | None, DataLoader | None][source]¶

Builds the train and test loaders in the correct order.

The training loader is always built first so that the StandardScaler is fitted before the test loader requests it. Shuffling is enabled for training and disabled for testing.

Returns:

A 2-tuple (train_loader, test_loader). Either element may be None if the corresponding split failed to load; callers should check for None before iterating.

Return type:

tuple[DataLoader | None, DataLoader | None]

class data_preparation.modules.uea_data_loaders.UEADataset(name: str, data_path: Path, split: str, scaler: StandardScaler | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset

Map-style PyTorch Dataset for a single UEA/UCR split.

Downloads (if necessary) and caches the requested dataset via sktime, transposes the array to time-first order (B, T, C), applies StandardScaler normalisation, and integer-encodes string class labels with LabelEncoder.

The scaler is fitted on the training split and must be supplied for the test split to prevent data leakage. Use UEADataFactory to manage this automatically.

name¶

UEA/UCR dataset identifier passed directly to sktime.datasets.load_UCR_UEA_dataset().

Type:

str

data_path¶

Directory where sktime caches downloaded datasets.

Type:

Path

split¶

One of "train" or "test".

Type:

str

scaler¶

Pre-fitted scaler for the "test" split. Must be None for "train". The scaler will be fitted and stored automatically. Defaults to None.

Type:

StandardScaler | None

get_scaler() → StandardScaler[source]¶

Returns the fitted scaler for use when constructing the test split.

Returns:

The StandardScaler fitted on the flattened training windows.

Return type:

StandardScaler

Raises:

ValueError – If called before the scaler has been fitted (i.e. on a "test" split instance or after a failed load).

data_preparation.modules.weather_labeling¶

Electric Truck Weather Labeling Module.

This module segments verified electric truck trip datasets into time or distance windows, fetches hourly weather observations from Meteostat for each window, classifies weather conditions and air temperatures using meteorological thresholds, and merges the resulting labels back into the original trip DataFrames.

Example

>>> labeler = TruckDataWeatherLabeler(cfg)
>>> labeler.run()
class data_preparation.modules.weather_labeling.TruckDataWeatherLabeler(cfg: DictConfig)[source]¶

Bases: object

Labels electric truck trip data with weather and air temperature conditions.

Segments verified trip files into time or distance windows, fetches hourly weather data from Meteostat for each window, classifies weather conditions using meteorological thresholds, and merges the labels back into the original trip DataFrame.

cfg¶

Hydra configuration object. Must contain a data_preparation sub-config with the fields``verified_data_dir``, file_extension, labeled_data_dir, results_dir, window_size_km, and window_size_seconds.

Type:

DictConfig

verified_data_dir¶

Directory containing verified parquet trip files.

Type:

Path

file_extension¶

File suffix used when globbing trip files (e.g. '.parquet').

Type:

str

labeled_data_dir¶

Directory where labeled parquet files are written.

Type:

Path

results_dir¶

Directory where label distribution JSON files are saved.

Type:

Path

window_size_km¶

Window size in kilometres for distance-based windowing.

Type:

float

window_size_seconds¶

Window size in seconds for time-based windowing.

Type:

int

weather_labeling_signals¶

Signal columns used for weather labeling.

Type:

list[str]

weather_codes¶

Mapping from Meteostat condition codes to weather labels.

Type:

dict[int, str]

create_windows(df: DataFrame, use_distance: bool = False, use_time: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]¶

Segments a trip DataFrame into fixed-size windows by distance or time.

Assigns a window_id to each row and marks the first, middle, and last row of every window with boolean flags.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame to segment. Must contain hirestotalvehdist_cval_icuc if use_distance=True.

  • use_distance (bool, optional) – If True, windows are defined by window_size_km driven distance. Defaults to False.

  • use_time (bool, optional) – If True, windows are defined by window_size_seconds row count. Defaults to True.

Returns:

Input DataFrame with four additional columns:

  • window_id – integer window index per row.

  • is_first_row – True for the first row of each window.

  • is_middle_row – True for the middle row of each window.

  • is_last_row – True for the last row of each window.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Raises:

ValueError – If both or neither of use_distance and use_time are True.

static dew_point(temp: float, rhum: float) → float[source]¶

Computes the dew point temperature from air temperature and relative humidity.

Uses the Magnus formula as a fallback when dew point is not directly available from the weather station.

Parameters:

  • temp (float) – Air temperature in degrees Celsius.

  • rhum (float) – Relative humidity as a percentage (0–100).

Returns:

Dew point temperature in degrees Celsius.

Return type:

float

References

Papers

Lawrence, M. G., 2005: The Relationship between Relative Humidity and the Dewpoint Temperature in Moist Air: A Simple Conversion and Applications. Bull. Amer. Meteor. Soc., 86, 225–234, https://doi.org/10.1175/BAMS-86-2-225.

Alduchov, O. A., and R. E. Eskridge, 1996: Improved Magnus Form Approximation of Saturation Vapor Pressure. J. Appl. Meteor. Climatol., 35, 601–609, https://journals.ametsoc.org/view/journals/apme/35/4/1520-0450_1996_035_0601_imfaos_2_0_co_2.xml.

fetch_hourly_weather_data(df: DataFrame, time_delta_hour: int = 5) → DataFrame[source]¶

Fetches hourly weather data from Meteostat for each window in the trip.

For each window, attempts to fetch weather data using the middle, first, or last row GPS point as a fallback chain. If all attempts fail, a row of NaN values is inserted. Missing temperature values are filled from the onboard sensor (airtempoutsd_cval_cpc) and missing dew points are computed via dew_point().

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Windowed trip DataFrame. Must contain signal_time, window_id, is_middle_row, is_first_row, is_last_row, latitude_cval_ippc, longitude_cval_ippc, altitude_cval_ippc, and airtempoutsd_cval_cpc columns.

  • time_delta_hour (int, optional) – Hours added/subtracted around the window timestamp to define the Meteostat query interval. Defaults to 5.

Returns:

One row per window with the following columns:

  • window_id – window index.

  • airtempoutsd_cval_cpc – onboard air temperature sensor value.

  • temp (°C) – air temperature.

  • dwpt (°C) – dew point temperature.

  • rhum (%) – relative humidity.

  • prcp (mm) – one-hour precipitation total.

  • snow (mm) – snow depth.

  • wdir (°) – average wind direction.

  • wspd (km/h) – average wind speed.

  • wpgt (km/h) – peak wind gust.

  • pres (hPa) – sea-level air pressure.

  • tsun (min) – one-hour sunshine total.

  • coco (int) – Meteostat weather condition code.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

static fill_matching_gaps(series: Series, max_gap_size: int = 3) → Series[source]¶

Fills small NaN gaps in a categorical Series by safe interpolation.

A gap is filled only when both the preceding and following non-NaN values are identical, ensuring no label is invented at boundaries between different conditions.

Parameters:

  • series (pd.Series) – Categorical Series with potential NaN gaps.

  • max_gap_size (int, optional) – Maximum number of consecutive NaN values to fill. Gaps larger than this are left unchanged. Defaults to 3.

Returns:

Copy of series with qualifying gaps filled.

Return type:

pd.Series

get_datasets() → tuple[list[Path], list[str]][source]¶

Gets all trip files in the verified data directory.

Returns:

A tuple of (paths, filenames) where

paths is a sorted list of pathlib.Path objects matching file_extension, and filenames contains the corresponding stems (no extension).

Return type:

tuple[list[Path], list[str]]

static label_truck_data(truck_data: DataFrame, weather_data_labeled: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]¶

Merges weather labels into the original truck trip DataFrame.

Joins air_temperature and weather columns from the labeled weather DataFrame onto the truck data using window_id as the key, then drops the temporary window_id and is_middle_row columns.

Parameters:

  • truck_data (pd.DataFrame) – Original trip DataFrame containing a window_id column.

  • weather_data_labeled (pd.DataFrame) – Labeled weather DataFrame as returned by label_weather_data(). Must contain window_id, air_temperature, and weather columns.

Returns:

Truck DataFrame with air_temperature and

weather columns added and window_id and is_middle_row dropped.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

label_weather_data(df: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]¶

Assigns weather and air temperature labels to each window using meteorological thresholds.

Classification follows a priority chain: Meteostat code → storm → heavy snow → snow → heavy rain → rain → high wind → fog → clear. Small unlabeled gaps are filled by fill_matching_gaps() when surrounding labels agree.

Parameters:

df (pd.DataFrame) – Windowed weather DataFrame. Must contain airtempoutsd_cval_cpc, dwpt, rhum, prcp, snow, wspd, wpgt, pres, tsun, and coco columns.

Returns:

Input DataFrame with three additional columns:

  • air_temperature – air temperature label (e.g. 'cold', 'warm').

  • weather – weather condition label (e.g. 'rain', 'fog').

  • weather_gap_filled – True where a gap was filled rather

than directly observed.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

run() → None[source]¶

Runs the full weather labeling pipeline over all verified trip files.

For each trip file, the pipeline:

  1. Creates time-based windows with create_windows()

  2. Fetches hourly weather data with fetch_hourly_weather_data()

  3. Labels weather conditions with label_weather_data()

  4. Merges labels into the trip data with label_truck_data()

  5. Saves the labeled parquet and label distribution JSON

Already-processed files are skipped. Logs total elapsed time on completion.

save_label_distribution(df_labeled: DataFrame, filename: str) → None[source]¶

Computes and saves label distributions for weather columns as JSON.

Calculates the percentage distribution of values in air_temperature and weather columns and saves the result to results_dir.

Parameters:

  • df_labeled (pd.DataFrame) – Labeled trip DataFrame containing air_temperature and weather columns.

  • filename (str) – Output filename stem (without extension).

setup_dirs() → None[source]¶

Creates required output directories if they do not already exist.

Next
layers
Previous
data_preparation
